chief_complaint: A 3-year-old female patient presents with nausea, fever, and pallor.
history: The patient has a history of iron deficiency anemia and recurrent pneumonia. Clinical symptoms include nausea, fever, and pallor. Initial evaluations indicated moderate anemia (HGB 73 g/L) and signs of infection, with imaging revealing diffuse ground-glass opacities.
exam: Vital signs: fever, tachycardia. Physical examination: pallor.
labs: Hemoglobin (HGB): 73 g/L, imaging: diffuse ground-glass opacities.
